It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of [[:en:Mori, Shizuoka|Mori]][http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near [[:en:Nissaka-shuku|Nissaka-shuku]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]] highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from [[:en:Sei Shōnagon|Sei Shōnagon]]'s ''[[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]]'', the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] and the [[:en:Jippensha Ikku|Jippensha Ikku]]'s ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

事任八幡宮 | image = File:Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 2.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Kotonomama Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.798389|N|138.075428|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} | type = | deity = Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto
[[Hachiman]] | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] | established = c.[[Kofun period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 642 Yasaka, Kakegawa-shi, Shizuoka-ken 436-0004 | website = {{Official|1=http://kotonomama.org}} | architecture_style = | festival = three days prior to [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] in September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa|WD=Q823988}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kotonomama Hachiman-gū'''|事任八幡宮||}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of [[:en:Mori, Shizuoka|Mori]][http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near [[:en:Nissaka-shuku|Nissaka-shuku]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]] highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from [[:en:Sei Shōnagon|Sei Shōnagon]]'s ''[[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]]'', the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] and the [[:en:Jippensha Ikku|Jippensha Ikku]]'s ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Hachiman]] | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] | established = c.[[Kofun period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 642 Yasaka, Kakegawa-shi, Shizuoka-ken 436-0004 | website = {{Official|1=http://kotonomama.org}} | architecture_style = | festival = three days prior to [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] in September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa|WD=Q823988}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kotonomama Hachiman-gū'''|事任八幡宮||}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the city of [[:en:Kakegawa, Shizuoka|Kakegawa]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of [[:en:Mori, Shizuoka|Mori]][http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to [[:en:Respect for the Aged Day|Respect for the Aged Day]] in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]] (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near [[:en:Nissaka-shuku|Nissaka-shuku]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō (road)|Tōkaidō]] highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from [[:en:Sei Shōnagon|Sei Shōnagon]]'s ''[[:en:The Pillow Book|The Pillow Book]]'', the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] and the [[:en:Jippensha Ikku|Jippensha Ikku]]'s ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|WD=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11371148]] Wikidata name: Kotonomama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kakegawa, Shizuoka, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kotonomama Hachiman-gū
事任八幡宮 | image = File:Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 2.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Kotonomama Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.798389|N|138.075428|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} | type = | deity = Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto
[[Hachiman]] | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} | established = c.[[Kofun period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 642 Yasaka, Kakegawa-shi, Shizuoka-ken 436-0004 | website = {{Official|1=http://kotonomama.org}} | architecture_style = | festival = three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa|WD=Q823988}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kotonomama Hachiman-gū'''|事任八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|en|Mori, Shizuoka|WD=Q1345571|lt=Mori}}[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|WD=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:11]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|WD=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category:11 commons]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11371148]] Wikidata name: Kotonomama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kakegawa, Shizuoka, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kotonomama Hachiman-gū
事任八幡宮 | image = File:Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 2.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Kotonomama Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.798389|N|138.075428|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} | type = | deity = Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto
[[Hachiman]] | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} | established = c.[[Kofun period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 642 Yasaka, Kakegawa-shi, Shizuoka-ken 436-0004 | website = {{Official|1=http://kotonomama.org}} | architecture_style = | festival = three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa|WD=Q823988}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kotonomama Hachiman-gū'''|事任八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|en|Mori, Shizuoka|WD=Q1345571|lt=Mori}}[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|WD=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category:11 commons]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|WD=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category:11 commons]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kakegawa, Shizuoka, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kotonomama Hachiman-gū
事任八幡宮 | image = File:Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 2.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Kotonomama Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.798389|N|138.075428|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} | type = | deity = Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto
[[Hachiman]] | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} | established = c.[[Kofun period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 642 Yasaka, Kakegawa-shi, Shizuoka-ken 436-0004 | website = {{Official|1=http://kotonomama.org}} | architecture_style = | festival = three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa|WD=Q823988}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kotonomama Hachiman-gū'''|事任八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|en|Mori, Shizuoka|WD=Q1345571|lt=Mori}}[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|WD=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category:11 commons]] [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kakegawa, Shizuoka, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kotonomama Hachiman-gū
事任八幡宮 | image = File:Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 2.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Kotonomama Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.798389|N|138.075428|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} | type = | deity = Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto
[[Hachiman]] | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} | established = c.[[Kofun period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 642 Yasaka, Kakegawa-shi, Shizuoka-ken 436-0004 | website = {{Official|1=http://kotonomama.org}} | architecture_style = | festival = three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa|WD=Q823988}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kotonomama Hachiman-gū'''|事任八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|en|Mori, Shizuoka|WD=Q1345571|lt=Mori}}[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}} (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|WD=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Qq]]

This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū]][[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]][[ja:事任八幡宮]][[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kakegawa, Shizuoka, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kotonomama Hachiman-gū
事任八幡宮 | image = File:Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 2.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Kotonomama Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.798389|N|138.075428|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767|1=Shinto}} | type = | deity = Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto
[[Hachiman]] | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} | established = c.[[Kofun period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 642 Yasaka, Kakegawa-shi, Shizuoka-ken 436-0004 | website = {{Official|1=http://kotonomama.org}} | architecture_style = | festival = three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|1=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa|WD=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kotonomama Hachiman-gū'''|事任八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|WD=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|en|Mori, Shizuoka|WD=Q1345571|lt=Mori|1=Mori, Shizuoka}}[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|1=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|WD=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku|1=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon|1=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book|1=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku|1=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|1=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|en|Mori, Shizuoka|WD=Q1345571|lt=Mori|1=Mori, Shizuoka}}[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|WD=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|1=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|WD=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku|1=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|WD=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|WD=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon|1=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|WD=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book|1=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|WD=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku|1=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|WD=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|1=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|WD=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

事任八幡宮 | image = File:Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 2.jpg | caption = ''Heiden'' of Kotonomama Hachiman-gū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.798389|N|138.075428|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}} | type = | deity = Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto
[[Hachiman]] | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} | established = c.[[Kofun period]] | date_destroyed = | location = 642 Yasaka, Kakegawa-shi, Shizuoka-ken 436-0004 | website = {{Official|1=http://kotonomama.org}} | architecture_style = | festival = three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|qid=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|1=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kakegawa|qid=Q823988|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kotonomama Hachiman-gū'''|事任八幡宮||}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the city of {{ill|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|en|Kakegawa, Shizuoka|qid=Q823988|lt=Kakegawa|1=Kakegawa, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is one of two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|qid=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, the other being the [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni Jinja]] in the town of {{ill|Mori, Shizuoka|en|Mori, Shizuoka|qid=Q1345571|lt=Mori|1=Mori, Shizuoka}}[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''," p. 1.]; retrieved 2011-08-010 The shrine's main festival is held annually three days prior to {{ill|Respect for the Aged Day|en|Respect for the Aged Day|qid=Q1186127|lt=Respect for the Aged Day|1=Respect for the Aged Day}} in September.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The main ''[[kami]]'' at this shrine is {{nihongo|'''Kotonomachi-hime no mikoto'''|己等乃麻知媛命||}}, the mother of [[Ame-no-Koyane]] and the ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and the [[Fujiwara clan]]. However, as the one of the shrine implies, the main focus of the shrine is on the [[Hachiman]] trinity, which at this shrine are identified as {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku `Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=4569669301 |language=Japanese}} ==History== The foundation date of this shrine is unknown. The shrine's legend states that it was established by the [[Mononobe clan]], who were the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' during the reign of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}} (131-190 AD), but nothing is known of its history prior to its appearance in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. The shrine also claims that it was originally located on Mount Hongu to the north of the present site, and was relocated in 807 by [[Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]. As the worship of [[Hachiman]] spread in the late Heian period, [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] had a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] installed at this shrine in 1062, which was thereafter referred to as the {{nihongo|Nissaka Hachiman-gu|日坂八幡宮||}} or simply {{nihongo|Hachiman-gu|八幡宮||}}. It has been referred to as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tōtōmi Province since the early Kamakura period. As the shrine of located near {{ill|Nissaka-shuku|en|Nissaka-shuku|qid=Q956512|lt=Nissaka-shuku|1=Nissaka-shuku}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|qid=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} highway near the location of the feared Nakayama Pass, one of the three major obstacles on the Tōkaidō, travelers would make prayers and offerings at this shrine. This is mentioned many times in literature, from {{ill|Sei Shōnagon|en|Sei Shōnagon|qid=Q231603|lt=Sei Shōnagon|1=Sei Shōnagon}}'s ''{{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|qid=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book|1=The Pillow Book}}'', the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|qid=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}}'' of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} and the {{ill|Jippensha Ikku|en|Jippensha Ikku|qid=Q260290|lt=Jippensha Ikku|1=Jippensha Ikku}}'s ''{{ill|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|en|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|qid=Q1279587|lt=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|1=Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige}}'' of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|qid=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} [[de:Kotonomama Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:事任八幡宮]] [[zh:事任八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11371148}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

It was restored by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in 1603, who gave it estates worth 100 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|qid=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' for its upkeep. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], it was given the Rank Prefectural shrine under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. The ancient name of "Kotonomama" was only revived after the end of {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku `Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=4582945619 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kotonomama Hachimangu 201708late 5.jpg|Second Torii 事任八幡宮 拝殿.jpg|Heiden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== [[Category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} *[http://kotonomama.org Official website] *[https://www.city.kakegawa.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/docs/8724.html Kakegawa City home page] {{Ichinomiya}} {{Commons category|Kotonomama Hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q11371148|co|commons:Category:Kotonomama Hachimangū|de|Kotonomama Hachiman-gū|en|Kotonomama Hachiman-gū|ja|事任八幡宮|zh|事任八幡宮}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kakegawa, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
